findings.title: Remediate Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
findings.description: Address the 15% drop in search relevance scores for anonymous users caused by the new ranking algorithm in v2.4.1.
assumptions: The new ranking algorithm can be adjusted to improve relevance for anonymous users.

evidence.excerpt: Our p50 search relevance score fell from 0.85 to 0.72 immediately following the v2.4.1 release.

recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Implement changes to improve relevance for anonymous users.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new ranking algorithm is deployed WHEN an anonymous user performs a search THEN the search relevance score should be >= 0.80.

findings.title: Reduce Support Ticket Volume Due to Irrelevant Results
findings.description: Implement changes to the search algorithm to reduce the volume of support tickets related to irrelevant search results.
assumptions: Users will experience fewer irrelevant results after algorithm adjustments.

evidence.excerpt: Support volume has spiked 3x since v2.4.1 rolled out.

recommendations: Monitor support ticket volume post-implementation.
Gather user feedback on search results.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the search algorithm is improved WHEN users perform searches THEN support ticket volume should decrease by 50% within 4 weeks.

findings.title: Optimize Mobile Search Latency
findings.description: Profile and optimize the search algorithm to reduce mobile search latency from 890ms to below 500ms.
assumptions: The current algorithm can be optimized for mobile performance.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms.

recommendations: Conduct performance profiling of the search algorithm.
Implement caching strategies for mobile.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the search algorithm is optimized WHEN a mobile user performs a search THEN the P95 latency should be <= 500ms.

findings.title: Fix Stale Autocomplete Suggestions
findings.description: Adjust the cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions to ensure users receive up-to-date suggestions.
assumptions: The cache TTL can be modified without impacting performance.

evidence.excerpt: Autocomplete suggestions are sourced from a cache refreshed every 24 hours.

recommendations: Set the cache TTL back to 24 hours.
Test the autocomplete feature for freshness.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the cache TTL is set to 24 hours WHEN a user requests autocomplete suggestions THEN suggestions should reflect current product availability.

findings.title: Implement Instrumentation for New Search Features
findings.description: Add analytics instrumentation for image and voice search features to track their performance and user engagement.
assumptions: Instrumentation can be added without impacting existing features.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Define and implement analytics events for image and voice search.
Monitor adoption rates post-implementation.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new analytics events are implemented WHEN a user performs an image or voice search THEN the events should be logged correctly in the analytics system.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: Define and establish baseline metrics for image search to measure performance effectively.
assumptions: Baseline metrics can be established quickly after instrumentation.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Collect data for baseline metrics over a defined period.
Analyze the data to inform future improvements.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the image search is active WHEN data is collected for 30 days THEN baseline metrics should be established for query volume, zero-result rate, and click-through rate.

findings.title: Monitor Search Relevance Score
findings.description: Implement monitoring for the Search Relevance Score to track changes and identify issues proactively.
assumptions: Monitoring tools can be integrated with existing systems.

evidence.excerpt: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score.

recommendations: Set up alerts for significant drops in the Search Relevance Score.
Regularly review score trends.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN monitoring is set up WHEN the Search Relevance Score drops by more than 0.05 THEN an alert should be triggered.

findings.title: Establish Guardrail Metrics for Session Abandonment
findings.description: Define and monitor guardrail metrics for session abandonment rate after search to ensure user satisfaction.
assumptions: Guardrail metrics can be effectively monitored.

evidence.excerpt: The Session Abandonment Rate After Search measures the percentage of sessions where users abandon the session after performing a search.

recommendations: Set thresholds for acceptable session abandonment rates.
Implement monitoring and alerting for breaches.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN guardrail metrics are established WHEN the session abandonment rate exceeds 10% THEN an alert should be triggered.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection on Search Results
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on search results to inform future improvements.
assumptions: Users will provide feedback if prompted.

evidence.excerpt: Users are reporting that top results no longer match their intent.

recommendations: Design a feedback collection interface.
Analyze feedback to identify trends.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is implemented WHEN a user provides feedback on search results THEN the feedback should be recorded and categorized.

findings.title: Conduct A/B Testing for Search Algorithm Changes
findings.description: Run A/B tests on changes made to the search algorithm to evaluate their impact on relevance and user satisfaction.
assumptions: A/B testing framework is in place.

evidence.excerpt: The pre-launch A/B test ran for 14 days with a 50/50 traffic split.

recommendations: Design A/B tests for proposed algorithm changes.
Analyze results to inform further adjustments.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN A/B testing is conducted WHEN changes are made to the search algorithm THEN results should show a statistically significant improvement in relevance scores.

summary: The analysis of the recent deployment of SearchBoost revealed critical issues affecting search relevance, particularly for anonymous users. A structured backlog has been created to address these issues through a series of prioritized requirements, including algorithm adjustments, performance optimizations, and enhanced user feedback mechanisms. Each requirement is designed to improve user satisfaction and engagement with the search functionality.
